instructions.instruction: ALWAYS run `vault-cli consolidate --propose` before `vault-cli consolidate --apply`; never skip the propose step
instructions.relevant_when: Any consolidation workflow; user asks to consolidate, summarise, or clean up memories
instructions.why_given: Applying without proposing bypasses human review of memory merges, which can destroy important context irreversibly

instructions.instruction: After running --propose, instruct the user to review and approve proposals in Obsidian before running --apply
original_snippets: user reviews in Obsidian, sets status: approved or rejected
instructions.relevant_when: After --propose completes and before --apply is run
instructions.why_given: The human approval step is mandatory; the agent must not auto-apply without human review

instructions.instruction: Use `--project <id>` with --propose to limit consolidation to a specific project scope
instructions.relevant_when: User wants to consolidate memories for one project only
instructions.why_given: Without scoping, all projects' episodic memories are consolidated together

instructions.instruction: After --apply, source episodic memories are marked 'superseded' but never deleted
original_snippets: marks source episodics as 'superseded'. Source episodics are NEVER deleted
instructions.relevant_when: User asks what happens to old memories after consolidation
instructions.why_given: The agent must not reassure users that episodics are deleted — they are preserved

instructions.instruction: Human-edited memories are never overwritten by consolidation
original_snippets: Human edits are always respected, never overwritten
instructions.relevant_when: User asks about safety of running consolidation on memories they have manually edited
instructions.why_given: Human-edited memories have special immunity to automated changes

instructions.instruction: Consolidation produces semantic memories from clusters of episodic memories
original_snippets: Clusters episodic memories into semantic ones
instructions.why_given: The agent must understand the output tier so it can explain the process accurately
